The **1986–87 NCAA Division I men's ice hockey season** began in October 1986 and concluded with the [1987 NCAA Division I Men's Ice Hockey Tournament](/wiki/1987_NCAA_Division_I_Men%27s_Ice_Hockey_Tournament "1987 NCAA Division I Men's Ice Hockey Tournament")'s championship game on March 28, 1987 at the [Joe Louis Arena](/wiki/Joe_Louis_Arena "Joe Louis Arena") in [Detroit, Michigan](/wiki/Detroit "Detroit"). This was the 40th [season](/wiki/List_of_NCAA_Division_I_men%27s_ice_hockey_seasons "List of NCAA Division I men's ice hockey seasons") in which an NCAA ice hockey championship was held and is the 93rd year overall where an NCAA school fielded a team.

Notre Dame and Kent State formed the [ACHA](/wiki/American_Collegiate_Hockey_Association_(1986%E2%80%9389) "American Collegiate Hockey Association (1986–89)") with two other schools, however, the two other universities didn't play at the [Division I](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I "NCAA Division I") level and its standings are not official.
